title: Manufacturing expands as ‘big leap’ puts prices at post-2022 high: ISM
summary:
In March, manufacturing activity in the U.S. experienced a notable increase, marking the third consecutive month of growth. The Institute for Supply Management (ISM) reported a significant rise in the prices manufacturers are paying for inputs, which surged to 78.3%, up from 70.5% in February. This increase is attributed to various factors, including geopolitical tensions, particularly the ongoing conflict involving Iran, which has been cited by 40% of survey respondents as a concern affecting their operations. Additionally, 20% of the comments referenced the impact of tariffs, indicating a complex landscape for manufacturers navigating both domestic and international challenges.

The ISM's findings highlight a growing concern among manufacturers regarding inflationary pressures. With more than two-thirds of comments from respondents being negative, the sentiment reflects unease about the economic outlook. The war in Iran has been identified as a new variable influencing business operations, alongside uncertainties related to U.S. economic policy. Despite a recent Supreme Court ruling that struck down certain tariffs, the overall environment remains fraught with challenges that could impact manufacturing stability.

Economists are forecasting a surge in headline inflation, projected to reach 4.2% this year, significantly exceeding the Federal Reserve's target. The conflict has driven energy prices up sharply, with Brent crude oil prices soaring approximately 44% since the onset of hostilities. This increase in energy costs is expected to exert upward pressure on both headline and core inflation metrics, complicating the Federal Reserve's monetary policy decisions as they navigate these turbulent economic conditions.
social_text: Manufacturing activity rose for the third month, with prices paid by manufacturers hitting 78.3%, the highest since June 2022. The Iran war has driven energy prices up 44%, pushing inflation forecasts to 4.2%.

title: Marvell Technology Is Partnering with Nvidia on the Future of AI Infrastructure. Does That Make MRVL Stock a Buy Here?
summary:
Marvell Technology has entered a significant partnership with Nvidia, marking a pivotal moment in the race to develop AI infrastructure. This collaboration is underscored by Nvidia's substantial $2 billion investment in Marvell, which has led to a notable surge in MRVL stock, increasing by nearly 13% and elevating its market capitalization to approximately $86.6 billion. The partnership centers around Nvidia's NVLink Fusion platform, a sophisticated system that enables companies to construct tailored AI infrastructure utilizing Nvidia's extensive ecosystem. Marvell will provide specialized accelerator chips and networking solutions that integrate seamlessly into this architecture, enhancing the overall capabilities of AI compute systems.

The strategic alliance is not just about hardware; it also encompasses advancements in silicon photonics technology, which is crucial for the future of telecommunications. Both companies aim to transform global telecom networks into AI infrastructure, leveraging Nvidia's Aerial AI-RAN technology for next-generation networks. Nvidia's CEO, Jensen Huang, emphasized the significance of this partnership, stating that it empowers customers to harness Nvidia's AI infrastructure ecosystem effectively. Marvell's CEO, Matt Murphy, echoed this sentiment, highlighting the synergy between Marvell's expertise in optical digital signal processing and Nvidia's AI ecosystem.

As Marvell continues to grow its footprint in the semiconductor industry, this partnership positions it as a key player in the burgeoning AI infrastructure market. With the increasing demand for AI capabilities across various sectors, the collaboration with Nvidia could serve as a catalyst for further growth and innovation. Investors are keenly observing how this deal will impact MRVL stock and whether it will propel the company to new heights in the competitive landscape of AI technology.
social_text: Marvell Technology partners with Nvidia in a $2 billion deal to enhance AI infrastructure, boosting MRVL stock by 13% to $86.6 billion market cap. The collaboration focuses on custom silicon and networking solutions for AI compute systems.

title: Anthropic took down thousands of GitHub repos trying to yank its leaked source code — a move the company says was an accident
summary:
Anthropic, a prominent AI company, faced a significant backlash after accidentally taking down thousands of GitHub repositories while attempting to remove leaked source code for its Claude Code application. The incident occurred when a software engineer discovered that the source code had been inadvertently included in a recent release, leading to widespread sharing among AI enthusiasts on GitHub. In response, Anthropic issued a takedown notice under U.S. digital copyright law, which affected approximately 8,100 repositories, including legitimate forks of its own publicly available code. This unintended consequence sparked outrage among developers whose projects were impacted by the sweeping action.

Boris Cherny, the head of Claude Code at Anthropic, clarified that the takedown was an accident and that the company had retracted most of the notices, limiting the action to just one repository and 96 forks that contained the leaked code. The spokesperson explained that the notice inadvertently reached more repositories than intended due to the interconnected nature of the fork network associated with their public Claude Code repository. Following the retraction, GitHub restored access to the affected repositories, alleviating some of the frustration among developers.

This incident adds to the challenges Anthropic faces as it prepares for a potential IPO, a process that requires meticulous attention to compliance and execution. The leak of source code, coupled with the botched takedown, raises concerns about the company's operational oversight and could lead to shareholder lawsuits. As Anthropic navigates these turbulent waters, the incident serves as a reminder of the complexities and risks associated with managing proprietary technology in the fast-evolving AI landscape.
social_text: Anthropic accidentally took down 8,100 GitHub repos while trying to remove leaked source code for Claude Code. The company retracted most notices, affecting only one repo and 96 forks, as it prepares for an IPO.

title: Yotta sues Evolve again
summary:
Yotta Technologies has filed a new lawsuit against Evolve Bank & Trust in California state court, alleging that the bank engaged in a Ponzi-like scheme that resulted in the theft of millions from Yotta's customers. This lawsuit, filed on March 13, follows a series of legal actions initiated by Yotta since May 2024, when users were locked out of their accounts and unable to access approximately $80 million in funds. The lawsuit claims that Evolve's actions have decimated Yotta's business, leading to a significant loss of value and damaging the company's reputation in the market.

The allegations against Evolve highlight a severe breach of duty, with Yotta asserting that the bank's misconduct has left its customers enraged and unwilling to continue using its services. Yotta's previous lawsuit in federal court was dismissed due to the involvement of Synapse Financial, a middleware company that connected Yotta and Evolve. In the latest filing, Yotta has included Synapse as a defendant, although it has stated that it does not intend to pursue claims against the company, doing so merely to comply with the federal court's ruling.

Yotta's legal battle underscores the challenges faced by fintech companies when partnering with traditional banks. The ongoing litigation not only seeks to recover lost funds for customers but also aims to restore Yotta's reputation and operational viability. As the case progresses, it will be crucial to monitor the implications for both Yotta and Evolve, as well as the broader fintech landscape, which is increasingly scrutinized for its partnerships and regulatory compliance.
social_text: Yotta Technologies has filed a lawsuit against Evolve Bank & Trust, alleging a Ponzi-like scheme that deprived users of $80 million. Yotta's reputation is severely damaged, and it seeks to restore its rights in court.

This performance has likely contributed to Wood's bullish stance on Circle, as she seeks to capitalize on its potential in the evolving financial landscape.

The investment in Circle comes at a time when many tech stocks are facing skepticism due to high valuations and profit-taking. title: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA): Billionaire Paul Tudor Jones Continues to Be Bullish
summary:
NVIDIA Corporation (NASDAQ: NVDA) continues to attract significant attention from investors, particularly billionaire Paul Tudor Jones, who has maintained a bullish stance on the stock for over 15 years. Recent filings reveal that Tudor Investment Corp has increased its holdings in NVIDIA to 2.1 million shares, marking an impressive 80% rise from the previous quarter. This sustained investment reflects confidence in NVIDIA's strategic direction and growth potential, especially as the company transitions from its Blackwell architecture to the newly introduced Rubin platform.

The Rubin platform, which commenced full production in early 2026, is anticipated to revolutionize NVIDIA's performance metrics, offering up to ten times more inference throughput per watt than its predecessor. CEO Jensen Huang has projected that the combined revenue from Blackwell and Rubin chips could reach $1 trillion by 2027. This ambitious forecast is bolstered by NVIDIA's foray into Agentic AI, which aims to develop autonomous systems capable of executing complex tasks independently, further enhancing the company's appeal to institutional investors.

In addition to its hardware advancements, NVIDIA has launched the NemoClaw software platform, designed to ensure security and privacy for enterprise AI agents. This move aligns with the evolving landscape of Software-as-a-Service, as NVIDIA positions itself at the forefront of the emerging Agent-as-a-Service economy. While some analysts suggest that other AI stocks may offer greater upside potential, NVIDIA's robust growth trajectory and strategic innovations continue to make it a focal point for hedge funds and investors alike.

title: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN): Billionaire Paul Tudor Jones Likes This Tech Stock

The driving force behind this bullish sentiment is Amazon Web Services (AWS), which reported a remarkable 24% year-over-year revenue increase to $35.6 billion in February 2026, marking its fastest growth rate in over three years. This growth is attributed to the sophistication of Amazon's AI-driven ad auction system, which boasts operating margins near 50%, compared to the much lower margins in retail. While some analysts suggest that other AI stocks may offer better risk-reward profiles, the overall outlook for Amazon remains strong, bolstered by its diverse revenue streams and innovative technology initiatives.

title: Bearish on Pfizer (PFE) as Big Dividend Fails to Mask the Growth Problem
summary:
Pfizer (PFE), a leading player in the pharmaceutical industry, is currently facing significant growth challenges despite a recent stock increase of over 16% in the past six months. This performance stands in stark contrast to the broader market, which has seen a decline of more than 2%. However, analysts remain bearish on Pfizer's long-term prospects due to looming patent expirations that threaten to erode a substantial portion of its revenue. By 2030, it is projected that Pfizer could lose approximately $15–20 billion in annual revenue, which constitutes about a third of its current revenue base. Key drugs such as Eliquis, Ibrance, Xtandi, and Vyndaqel are nearing their patent expiration dates, leading to concerns over a potential revenue cliff that could result in a prolonged decline in both revenue and earnings.

The anticipated loss of exclusivity for these major drugs is not merely a routine patent cycle; it represents a concentrated challenge that could position Pfizer among the weakest growth profiles in the large-cap pharmaceutical sector. Analysts predict that the company's revenue could contract at an annual rate of around 5% through the end of the decade, with earnings potentially declining even more sharply. This situation is exacerbated by the lack of visible replacements in Pfizer's pipeline, which appears misaligned in timing. Unlike previous cycles where new blockbuster drugs were ready to fill the gaps, Pfizer's current pipeline may not deliver timely solutions to offset the impending revenue losses.

While Pfizer does have promising assets in areas such as oncology, immunology, and obesity, the timing of these developments is critical. Many of these potential breakthroughs are expected to arrive too late to mitigate the financial impact of the patent expirations. As a result, despite the attractive dividend yield of approximately 6.25%, investors are advised to approach Pfizer with caution, as the underlying growth problems may overshadow short-term gains. The combination of a structural revenue cliff and a poorly timed pipeline raises serious concerns about the company's ability to sustain its market position in the coming years.
social_text: Pfizer faces a looming revenue cliff with $15-20B at risk by 2030 due to patent expirations of key drugs like Eliquis and Ibrance, leading to potential 5% annual revenue decline amid pipeline timing issues.

title: Canada’s Cargojet plays role in CEO exit at Amazon partner 21 Air
summary:
The recent leadership changes at 21 Air, a partner of Cargojet, have raised eyebrows in the aviation industry. Fernando Alvarado, who previously served as managing director for Cargojet in the Americas, has stepped into the role of Chief Operating Officer at 21 Air. This transition comes after the reported departure of CEO Strauss, who had a brief tenure at the company following a challenging period at Amerijet. The leadership shake-up appears to be part of a broader strategy by Cargojet's new CEO, Pauline Dhillon, to infuse younger leadership into the organization, aiming for long-term growth and stability in a competitive market.

Sources indicate that the relationship between Cargojet and 21 Air may be under scrutiny, particularly concerning U.S. regulations that limit foreign control of U.S. airlines. The appointment of Alvarado, who has a history with Cargojet, could signal a strategic alignment between the two companies, although the exact nature of their partnership remains unclear. The operational base of 21 Air is in Miami, while its headquarters is in Greensboro, North Carolina, adding complexity to its corporate structure.

As the aviation sector continues to evolve, the changes at 21 Air reflect a broader trend of companies seeking innovative leadership to navigate the challenges ahead. With Cargojet's recent growth and the opening of new opportunities, the focus on developing a leadership team that can sustain this momentum is critical. The industry will be watching closely to see how these changes impact 21 Air's operations and its relationship with Cargojet moving forward.

title: Oracle reportedly joins tech peers in layoff spree
summary:
Oracle Corporation, a leading software giant, is reportedly undergoing significant layoffs as part of a broader trend in the tech industry where companies are trimming their workforce amidst increased investments in artificial intelligence (AI). Although Oracle has not disclosed the exact number of employees affected, reports suggest that around 10,000 positions may be eliminated. This decision follows a pattern seen across the tech sector, where firms like Meta have also recently laid off hundreds of workers. The layoffs at Oracle appear to be a strategic move to realign the company’s resources with its current business needs, particularly in light of advancements in AI technology.

The layoffs were communicated to employees via an early-morning email, which indicated that the decision was made after careful consideration of Oracle's organizational requirements. This restructuring is part of a larger initiative to enhance efficiency and productivity within the company. Oracle's fiscal third-quarter results highlighted the growing efficiency of AI models, particularly those capable of generating computer code, prompting the company to reorganize its product development teams into smaller, more agile units.

As Oracle joins its tech peers in this layoff trend, the implications for the workforce and the industry at large are significant. The shift towards AI-driven solutions is reshaping job roles and the overall employment landscape in technology. While these layoffs may be seen as a necessary step for companies to remain competitive, they also raise concerns about job security and the future of employment in the tech sector. The ongoing changes reflect a critical moment for both Oracle and the broader technology industry as they adapt to the evolving demands of the market.
social_text: Oracle is reportedly cutting around 10,000 jobs as part of a strategic shift towards AI efficiency, joining other tech giants like Meta in workforce reductions to realign resources and enhance productivity.

title: Commodity Roundup- March’s Top Performers and Underperformers
summary:
In March 2026, the commodities market experienced significant fluctuations, driven largely by geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. Precious metals, which had seen a surge in prices earlier in the year, began to stabilize as demand waned. However, biofuels like ethanol saw a notable increase of 12.12%, while coal prices in Rotterdam surged by 16.17%. In contrast, U.S. natural gas prices only marginally increased by 0.10% as the market transitioned into a low-demand shoulder season, although European natural gas prices skyrocketed due to regional conflicts, with U.K. futures rising by 63% and Dutch futures by 58.8% in March alone.

The refining sector also experienced dramatic shifts, with gasoline crack spreads rising by 11.01% and distillate crack spreads soaring by an astonishing 83.53%. This was reflected in the prices of gasoline and heating oil futures, which increased by 39.73% and 62.51%, respectively. The heightened demand for distillate fuels was attributed to supply concerns surrounding Brent crude oil, which is more suitable for refining into these products due to its sulfur content. The price of WTI crude oil futures climbed to $119.48 per barrel, marking a 51.56% increase, while Brent crude saw a 43.69% rise.

The escalation of military conflict in the Middle East, particularly Iran's actions that blocked the Strait of Hormuz, a vital shipping route for oil and other commodities, has had a profound impact on energy prices. This regional crisis has not only affected crude oil prices but has also led to substantial gains across various commodities, including ethanol, coal, sugar, and cocoa futures, all of which posted double-digit percentage increases in March. The overall market dynamics reflect a complex interplay of geopolitical events and seasonal demand shifts, underscoring the volatility inherent in the commodities sector.
social_text: In March 2026, U.K. natural gas futures surged 63%, while Brent crude oil rose 43.69%. Ethanol increased 12.12% and coal in Rotterdam jumped 16.17%, driven by Middle Eastern tensions affecting supply chains.

title: The reputation of troubled YC startup Delve has gotten even worse
summary:
The compliance startup Delve is facing serious allegations regarding its no-code tool, Pathways, which is claimed to be a derivative of an open-source product called SimStudio. The whistleblower, known as DeepDelver, has accused Delve of misrepresenting the origins of Pathways, asserting that the tool was a modified version of SimStudio, created without proper attribution or licensing. This situation raises significant concerns about intellectual property rights and compliance, particularly for a company that markets itself as a compliance solution provider.

DeepDelver's allegations suggest that Delve's actions could constitute a violation of the Apache software license, which mandates that original developers receive credit for their work. The founder of Sim.ai, Emir Karabeg, confirmed that Delve had no licensing agreement with his company and expressed surprise at Delve's decision to market the tool as a standalone product. The irony is palpable, as Delve's business model hinges on compliance, yet it may have engaged in practices that contradict the very principles it promotes.

This controversy not only tarnishes Delve's reputation but also raises questions about the ethical standards within the startup ecosystem, particularly among Y Combinator alumni who often collaborate and support each other. As the situation unfolds, it remains to be seen how Delve will address these allegations and whether it can recover from this damaging scrutiny. The implications for both Delve and the broader tech community could be significant, especially regarding trust and transparency in software development and compliance practices.
social_text: Delve faces serious allegations of misappropriating Sim.ai's open-source tool, claiming it as their own. Sim.ai's CEO confirmed no licensing agreement existed, raising compliance concerns for Delve's business model.

title: The 'magic number' for a comfortable retirement just got bigger
summary:
According to the 2026 edition of Northwestern Mutual's financial planning survey, Americans now estimate they need $1.46 million to retire comfortably. This figure serves as a guidepost for retirement planning, reflecting the growing concerns about financial preparedness among non-retirees. John Roberts, executive vice president at Northwestern Mutual, emphasizes that this number is not a strict savings target but rather a benchmark that highlights the gap between expectations and reality in retirement savings. The survey, which included responses from 4,375 adults, reveals that nearly half of non-retirees feel unprepared for retirement, with many fearing they could outlive their savings.

The increase in the retirement magic number from previous years, where it was as low as $1.25 million in 2022, underscores the impact of cumulative inflation on retirement planning. As costs for long-term care and other expenses continue to rise, the financial landscape for retirees is becoming increasingly challenging. The survey results indicate a significant disconnect between the amount Americans believe they need for a comfortable retirement and the actual savings most have accumulated. For instance, the typical household aged 65-74 holds approximately $200,000 in retirement accounts, far below the newly established benchmark.

This growing concern about retirement readiness is compounded by the reality that many Americans do not have close to $1 million saved. While some financial planners suggest that not every retiree needs $1.46 million to live comfortably, the findings highlight the necessity for individuals to reassess their retirement strategies. The survey serves as a wake-up call for many, urging them to take proactive steps in their financial planning to ensure a secure retirement amidst rising living costs and inflationary pressures.
social_text: Americans now believe they need $1.46 million to retire comfortably, a significant increase from $1.25 million in 2022. Nearly half of non-retirees feel unprepared for retirement, with many fearing they could outlive their savings.

title: US manufacturing sector grows in March; supplier delivery performance deteriorates
summary:
In March, the U.S. manufacturing sector showed signs of growth, with the Institute for Supply Management reporting a rise in the manufacturing PMI to 52.7, up from 52.4 in February. This marks the third consecutive month of expansion, as a PMI reading above 50 indicates growth in manufacturing activity. Economists had anticipated a slight decline, forecasting a PMI of 52.5, making the actual increase a positive surprise for the sector. The uptick in manufacturing activity reflects a combination of increased customer demand and ongoing supply chain challenges.

However, the report also highlighted significant issues within the supply chain, particularly due to the ongoing U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran, which has led to shipping restrictions and delays. The supplier deliveries index rose to 58.9, indicating slower delivery times, which are typically associated with a robust economy but in this case suggest disruptions. The conflict has also contributed to a surge in global crude prices, which have increased by over 50% since late February, further complicating supply chain dynamics.

As a result of these supply chain disruptions, manufacturers are facing rising costs for inputs, with the prices paid measure climbing to 78.3, the highest level since June 2022. This increase in input costs is expected to contribute to inflationary pressures throughout the year, potentially influencing the Federal Reserve's monetary policy decisions. The Fed has maintained its benchmark interest rate in the 3.50%-3.75% range, with projections indicating that inflation concerns may delay any rate cuts until 2026, despite hopes for economic recovery in the manufacturing sector.
social_text: U.S. manufacturing PMI rose to 52.7 in March, marking three months of expansion. However, supplier delivery times worsened to 58.9, indicating supply chain disruptions amid rising input costs, with prices paid surging to 78.3.

title: 3 High-Yield Dividend Stocks to Buy in 2026 for Passive Income
summary:
Best Buy (BBY) has garnered attention on Wall Street with a consensus 'Moderate Buy' rating from 24 analysts, where seven recommend a 'Strong Buy' and one suggests a 'Strong Sell'. Despite a significant decline of approximately 74% year-to-date, BBY's average target price stands at $74.31, indicating a potential upside of 16%. The stock's highest price estimate of $90 suggests a possible 40% increase over the next year, making it an attractive option for investors looking for recovery potential in a challenging market environment.

The company's dividend payout ratio is 58.6%, allowing Best Buy to balance rewarding shareholders while retaining earnings for future growth. With a commendable history of increasing dividends for 22 consecutive years, Best Buy is on the verge of joining the ranks of Dividend Aristocrats. Best Buy's forward dividend yield of 6.1% is particularly appealing for a consumer electronics retailer, especially during times of economic uncertainty. The company not only sells a variety of electronics but also offers services that generate recurring revenue, such as installation and technical support. As investors seek safety and predictability in turbulent markets, Best Buy's robust dividend history and strategic positioning make it a compelling choice for those interested in passive income through high-yield dividend stocks.

title: Dan Ives Wants You to Buy the Dip in CrowdStrike Stock: ‘Cybersecurity Budgets’ Will Double
summary:
Dan Ives, a prominent analyst at Wedbush, is advocating for investors to consider buying the dip in CrowdStrike Holdings (CRWD) stock following a recent sell-off. Despite concerns regarding artificial intelligence (AI) disruptions and broader technology market weaknesses, Ives believes that the current market sentiment is overlooking the significant growth potential in the cybersecurity sector. He argues that the negative news surrounding CrowdStrike and similar companies is largely priced in, and the fundamental demand for cybersecurity solutions continues to strengthen, driven by the increasing threat landscape posed by AI-driven cyberattacks.

Ives projects that cybersecurity budgets are set to double, reflecting a growing recognition of the critical importance of robust cybersecurity measures in today’s digital landscape. He emphasizes that the projected $4 trillion in AI-driven capital expenditures will serve as a catalyst for growth in the sector. While acknowledging potential macroeconomic risks that could affect spending, Ives views the current market weakness as a temporary setback rather than a long-term trend reversal. This perspective positions CrowdStrike as a key beneficiary of the anticipated surge in cybersecurity investments.

CrowdStrike, known for its cloud-native endpoint protection and threat intelligence solutions, has a market capitalization of approximately $96.4 billion. The company has seen a modest 9.32% increase in share price over the past year, indicating sustained demand for its services despite recent volatility. social_text: Dan Ives of Wedbush urges investors to buy CrowdStrike (CRWD) stock, predicting a doubling of cybersecurity budgets amid a $4 trillion AI capex surge. Current market weakness is a temporary speed bump, not a trend reversal.

title: Private employer job growth held steady with 62,000 new roles in March
summary:
In March, US private employers added 62,000 jobs, marking a steady increase from the previous month, as reported by payroll processor ADP. This growth surpassed economists' expectations, who had predicted an addition of only 40,000 jobs following a revised gain of 66,000 in February. The job growth was primarily driven by small businesses and sectors such as education and health services, while manufacturing and trade, transportation, and utilities experienced declines in hiring. ADP's chief economist, Nela Richardson, noted that while overall hiring remains stable, certain industries, particularly healthcare, are seeing more favorable job growth.

Despite the positive job additions, the labor market faces several risks that could hinder future growth. The ongoing US-Israel conflict and its potential impact on the economy may make businesses more cautious about hiring. Additionally, a slowdown in the healthcare sector could further dampen job growth prospects. Recent government data has painted a concerning picture for the labor market, with February's hiring rate being the lowest since April 2020 and a rise in the unemployment rate to 4.4%. The Chicago Fed anticipates a slight increase in the jobless rate to 4.5% for March, with official Labor Department data set to be released soon.

Overall, while March's job growth reflects a steady trend, the underlying challenges in the labor market, including sector-specific declines and external geopolitical factors, may pose significant hurdles ahead. Pay growth remains solid, with job-switchers experiencing a 6.6% increase compared to the previous year, indicating that while job availability may be uneven, compensation for workers is still on the rise. The upcoming Labor Department report will be crucial in assessing the true state of the labor market and its implications for the broader economy.
social_text: In March, US private employers added 62,000 jobs, exceeding expectations of 40,000. Healthcare and education sectors drove growth, while manufacturing jobs declined. Job-switchers saw pay growth accelerate by 6.6%.

title: $1,000 and 1 Stock: This Is the Growth Play for the Long Term
summary:
Investing $1,000 in a single stock should focus on long-term growth rather than short-term gains. Warren Buffett exemplifies this strategy by identifying companies with strong compounding potential and holding them for extended periods. One standout option for such an investment is Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN), which has established itself as a leader in both e-commerce and cloud computing. While its online retail segment drives significant revenue, Amazon's profitability is primarily derived from its cloud services, particularly Amazon Web Services (AWS).

Amazon's growth strategy is multifaceted, with a strong emphasis on digital marketing within its e-commerce platform. This segment is rapidly expanding and offers high margins, positioning Amazon as a formidable player in the digital advertising space. Additionally, the company's investments in robotics, artificial intelligence, and automation are crucial for enhancing operational efficiency and profitability. Although these advancements may not immediately reflect in revenue figures, they are pivotal for long-term growth.

The cloud computing sector, especially AWS, is witnessing accelerated revenue growth, driven by increasing demand. Amazon is heavily investing in this area to capitalize on market opportunities. Furthermore, the company is exploring the development of its own AI chips and models, which could significantly enhance its technological capabilities. By owning proprietary technology, Amazon could improve its AI training processes, thereby solidifying its competitive edge in the tech landscape and ensuring sustained growth in the future.
social_text: Investing $1,000 in Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) taps into its e-commerce and AWS dominance. With rapid growth in digital marketing and AI advancements, Amazon is poised for long-term profitability and innovation.

title: Nike stock tumbles more than 13%, hits 11-year low as weakness in China, high oil prices weigh on outlook
summary:
Nike's stock experienced a significant decline of over 13%, reaching an 11-year low as the company grapples with multiple challenges impacting its financial outlook. The downturn is primarily attributed to a stalled recovery in China, where revenue fell by 11% in the latest quarter, compounded by a 27% drop in equipment sales. Additionally, external factors such as rising oil prices and geopolitical tensions in the Middle East are creating an unpredictable environment that could further affect consumer behavior and input costs. CFO Matthew Friend acknowledged these challenges, indicating that Nike anticipates a low single-digit revenue decline in 2026, with flat earnings expected.

Despite these setbacks, Nike reported adjusted earnings per share of $0.35, surpassing Wall Street's expectations of $0.31, and total revenue of $11.3 billion, slightly above analyst forecasts. However, when adjusted for currency impacts, revenue showed a 3% decline year-over-year. The company's direct-to-consumer segment, Nike Direct, is also facing pressures, and the outlook for the upcoming quarter suggests a revenue decline of 2% to 5%, with Greater China expected to see a staggering 20% drop in sales.

Analysts remain cautious about Nike's turnaround efforts under CEO Elliott Hill, noting that while there are signs of progress, the overall sentiment is muted due to the ongoing challenges. CFRA analyst Zachary Warring emphasized that the company is in the midst of a complex turnaround, which may explain the lack of enthusiasm among investors. As Nike navigates these turbulent waters, its ability to adapt to changing market conditions will be crucial for its recovery and long-term growth prospects.
social_text: Nike's stock plummeted over 13%, hitting an 11-year low, as revenue in Greater China fell 11% and geopolitical tensions weigh on outlook. Earnings per share beat estimates at $0.35, but revenue is expected to decline 2-5% next quarter.

title: Franklin Templeton to acquire CoinFund spinoff to expand crypto push
summary:
Franklin Templeton, a leading investment management firm with over $1.7 trillion in assets under management, has announced its acquisition of 250 Digital, a cryptocurrency investment unit that was spun out from CoinFund. This strategic move is part of Franklin Templeton's broader initiative to enhance its presence in the digital asset space, which has seen a surge in interest from traditional financial institutions. The acquisition aligns with a growing trend where established financial firms are increasingly investing in cryptocurrencies, particularly in light of favorable regulatory conditions that emerged during the Trump administration.

The newly formed entity, named Franklin Crypto, will be spearheaded by industry veteran Christopher Perkins, who will serve as the division's head. Seth Ginns has been appointed as the chief investment officer, both of whom will report to Sandy Kaul, Franklin Templeton's head of innovation. This leadership team is expected to leverage Franklin Templeton's existing capabilities in blockchain technology and venture investing to create a robust digital assets investment platform that caters to the evolving needs of investors.

The transaction is anticipated to close in the second quarter of 2026, pending standard conditions, including client approvals. This acquisition not only signifies Franklin Templeton's commitment to the cryptocurrency market but also highlights the increasing convergence of traditional finance and digital assets, as firms seek to capitalize on the growth potential of this emerging sector. With Franklin Crypto, the firm aims to position itself as a key player in the rapidly evolving landscape of digital investments.
social_text: Franklin Templeton acquires CoinFund spinoff 250 Digital to launch Franklin Crypto, enhancing its digital assets platform. Led by Christopher Perkins, the division aims to capitalize on the growing crypto market.

title: US Senators Float Reserve-Backed Bitcoin Mining Bill, Raising Nationalization Prospect
summary:
On March 30, 2026, Senators Bill Cassidy and Cynthia Lummis introduced the Mined in America Act, a significant legislative proposal aimed at establishing a federal certification program for domestic Bitcoin mining operations. This act seeks to create a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve, allowing certified miners to sell newly mined Bitcoin directly to the federal government in exchange for a capital gains tax exemption. The bill emphasizes the importance of domestic production and aims to reduce reliance on foreign-manufactured mining hardware, which the sponsors argue poses a national security risk. The Department of Commerce will oversee the certification process, with support from the National Institute of Standards and Technology and the Manufacturing Extension Partnership to foster domestic hardware development.

The Mined in America Act is poised to reshape the landscape of Bitcoin mining in the United States by introducing a procurement mechanism that could establish the federal government as a consistent buyer of domestically mined Bitcoin. This would create a demand floor for Bitcoin, a concept that is unprecedented in the realm of sovereign commodity acquisition. The bill is currently awaiting committee referral, with the Senate Commerce, Science, and Transportation Committee expected to conduct the initial review. This legislative effort aligns with Senator Lummis's broader vision for a structured reserve architecture for federal Bitcoin holdings, which she first proposed in the BITCOIN Act of 2025.

If enacted, the Mined in America Act could have far-reaching implications for the Bitcoin mining industry, potentially incentivizing domestic operations and fostering innovation in mining technology. The bill's focus on national security and economic independence reflects a growing recognition of the strategic importance of cryptocurrency in the global economy. As the Senate prepares to review the bill, stakeholders in the cryptocurrency space are closely monitoring its progress, as its passage could significantly influence the future of Bitcoin mining and federal involvement in the cryptocurrency market.
social_text: Senators Cassidy and Lummis introduced the Mined in America Act, establishing a federal certification for Bitcoin miners and a Treasury procurement channel, potentially creating a demand floor for BTC and reducing foreign reliance.

title: Telehealth giant Hims & Hers says its customer support system was hacked
summary:
Hims & Hers, a prominent telehealth company specializing in weight-loss drugs and sexual health prescriptions, has reported a significant data breach involving its third-party customer support platform. The breach occurred between February 4 and February 7, when hackers infiltrated the company's ticketing system and accessed a wealth of sensitive customer information. Although Hims & Hers has assured that medical records remain secure, the compromised data includes customer names, contact information, and potentially other personal details that were not fully disclosed in the breach notice filed with the California attorney general's office.

The company has indicated that the breach was a result of a social engineering attack, where hackers deceived employees into granting access to their systems. This method of attack has become increasingly common, as customer support systems are often targeted due to the valuable personal information they contain. Hims & Hers has not yet disclosed the number of individuals affected by the breach, but under California law, any breach involving 500 or more residents must be reported. This incident highlights the growing risks associated with data security in the telehealth sector, where customer trust is paramount.

As the investigation continues, Hims & Hers has not confirmed whether any ransom demands were made by the hackers. The incident raises concerns about the vulnerability of customer support systems across various industries, as they are increasingly seen as lucrative targets for cybercriminals. Companies must remain vigilant and enhance their security measures to protect sensitive customer data from similar attacks in the future.
social_text: Hims & Hers confirmed a data breach affecting its customer support system, with hackers accessing names and email addresses. The breach occurred between February 4-7, highlighting vulnerabilities in telehealth data security.

title: Automakers unveil new EVs for US market despite sales downturn
summary:
At the New York Auto Show, major automakers showcased their latest electric vehicles (EVs) despite a significant downturn in sales attributed to the elimination of a $7,500 EV tax credit. Companies like Kia and Subaru introduced new models aimed at capturing consumer interest in a challenging market. Kia plans to launch its affordable EV3 later this year, while Subaru presented the 'Getaway,' a three-row SUV designed for families, marking its fourth EV entry into the U.S. market. This unveiling comes at a time when EV sales have dipped, with recent statistics showing a decline from 9.6% to 6.5% of total U.S. sales, the lowest since early 2022, as consumer demand wanes without substantial incentives.

Industry leaders expressed optimism about the future of the EV market, despite current challenges. Kia's marketing vice president, Russell Wager, believes that the market will rebound within three to four years, driven by rising gasoline prices that could reignite consumer interest in electric vehicles. Meanwhile, GM has reintroduced the Chevrolet Bolt EV at a competitive starting price of $27,600, aiming to attract buyers in a tightening market. The Alliance for Automotive Innovation highlighted the stark contrast in EV sales, emphasizing the need for renewed consumer incentives to stimulate demand.

Executives from various automakers, including Nissan and Hyundai, acknowledged the current lack of natural demand for EVs, attributing recent sales figures to heavy incentives rather than organic consumer interest. Hyundai's CEO noted a slight uptick in EV sales correlating with rising fuel prices, particularly in California, suggesting that market dynamics rather than regulatory pressures may play a crucial role in the future of EV adoption. As automakers navigate this complex landscape, their commitment to electric vehicles remains steadfast, with hopes for a resurgence in consumer demand in the coming years.
social_text: At the New York Auto Show, Kia and Subaru unveiled new EVs despite a sales drop to 6.5% of U.S. market share. Rising gas prices may boost interest, but demand remains weak without incentives. GM's Bolt EV starts at $27,600.

title: How the SpaceX IPO Could Affect These Popular Nasdaq ETFs
summary:
Elon Musk's SpaceX is poised to make history with an anticipated initial public offering (IPO) valued at an astonishing $1.75 trillion, potentially becoming the largest IPO ever recorded. The company is rumored to target a June listing on the Nasdaq, which has generated significant excitement among retail investors. Unlike typical IPO allocations, SpaceX plans to offer up to 30% of its shares to retail investors, tripling the usual amount. This unprecedented move is expected to attract a large number of non-professional investors, eager to capitalize on the potential growth of the space industry, especially given Musk's track record with Tesla, which has seen a staggering 2,280% return over the past decade.

The implications of the SpaceX IPO extend beyond just retail investors; it could significantly impact several well-known exchange-traded funds (ETFs), particularly the Invesco QQQ ETF and the Invesco NASDAQ 100 ETF. Both funds track the Nasdaq-100 index, which includes the largest non-financial services stocks. The addition of SpaceX to this index would not only alter the composition of these ETFs but could also enhance their attractiveness to investors seeking exposure to high-growth sectors. The competition between Nasdaq and the New York Stock Exchange for high-profile IPOs like SpaceX is fierce, as both exchanges vie for prestige and the potential for increased trading revenue.

As SpaceX prepares for its IPO, the financial landscape is bracing for a shift that could redefine investment strategies. The anticipated listing is expected to draw significant media attention and investor interest, potentially reshaping the dynamics of the Nasdaq and influencing the broader market. With the prospect of SpaceX becoming a major player in the public markets, the excitement surrounding this IPO reflects a growing trend of interest in space exploration and technology, signaling a new era for investors looking to diversify their portfolios with innovative and high-potential companies.
social_text: Elon Musk's SpaceX is set for a record $1.75 trillion IPO, offering 30% of shares to retail investors. This move could reshape ETFs like Invesco QQQ and QQQM, enhancing their growth potential in the Nasdaq-100 index.

title: Super Micro Computer Stock Bounces on Easing Iran War Fears. Should You Buy SMCI Here?
summary:
Supermicro (SMCI) shares experienced a notable uptick on March 31, driven by reports suggesting a potential ceasefire in the Middle East, which alleviated concerns regarding escalating tensions and their impact on energy prices. This positive sentiment was further bolstered by Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's remarks indicating a cautious stance on inflation, suggesting no immediate need for interest rate hikes despite ongoing geopolitical supply shocks. However, despite this temporary surge, SMCI's stock remains significantly down, approximately 35% from its year-to-date peak, indicating underlying challenges that investors should consider.

Investors are advised to approach Supermicro shares with caution, as the company's fundamental outlook appears to be deteriorating, particularly heading into 2026. The latest quarterly results revealed a concerning 550-basis-point contraction in gross margins, dropping to a mere 6.3%. This decline is attributed to an unfavorable product mix and increasing competition in the AI server market. Additionally, the company faces internal control deficiencies that have raised red flags among investors, compounded by a high concentration risk with one customer accounting for over 60% of total revenue.

From a technical analysis perspective, the recent rise in SMCI shares does little to mitigate the structural damage reflected in its trading charts. The stock continues to trade well below key moving averages, indicating a persistent downtrend that is unlikely to reverse without a significant catalyst. Furthermore, the relative strength index remains in the low 30s, suggesting potential for further declines. Notably, insider trading activity has been absent, with no buy transactions recorded in the past six months, signaling a lack of confidence from those closest to the company's operations.

title: Expedia Group (EXPE): Artisan Mid Cap Value Fund’s Top Overall Contributor
summary:

summary:

This investment strategy aims to create a robust AI ecosystem centered around Nvidia's architecture, particularly through its NVLink Fusion platform and advancements in silicon photonics technology. title: This "AI Factory" Growth Stock Is Up 85% in 12 Months -- and It's Still Cheap

The company's infrastructure solutions group (ISG) has been a key contributor to this growth, generating $19.6 billion in revenue for the most recent quarter, marking a remarkable 73% increase year-over-year. This growth trajectory indicates that Dell is not just a legacy tech company but is evolving rapidly to meet modern technological demands.

Despite its impressive stock performance, Dell's valuation remains attractive, suggesting that there may still be room for further investment. The company’s dual business model, encompassing both its traditional client solutions group (CSG) and the rapidly expanding ISG, positions it well for sustained growth. As AI continues to reshape industries, Dell's strategic focus on AI infrastructure could lead to even greater financial success, making it a compelling option for investors looking to capitalize on the AI revolution.

title: Uber Holds a 5.82% Stake in WeRide. Should You Buy WRD Stock Too?

The collaboration between Uber and WeRide not only enhances Uber's service offerings but also positions WeRide to potentially recover from its recent performance slump. As the autonomous vehicle market continues to evolve, this partnership could yield significant benefits, making WeRide an attractive option for investors looking to capitalize on the growing demand for self-driving technology.

title: How wealthy investors are navigating the markets after the S&P 500's worst month in a year
summary:
The S&P 500 experienced its worst month since March 2025, driven by a combination of geopolitical tensions, rising oil prices, and persistent inflation. Investors, particularly those with high and ultra-high net worth, are using this volatility as an opportunity to strategically reposition their portfolios. Many are opting to buy dips and seek out individual stocks that may benefit from the current economic landscape, which includes the ongoing Iran war and the implications of rising oil costs on the U.S. economy. This approach reflects a long-term investment strategy rather than a reactionary one, as these investors are focused on generational wealth rather than short-term gains.

Members of R360, a network for ultra-high net worth individuals, are currently holding significant cash reserves, with some maintaining up to 30% of their portfolios in cash and short-duration debt. Barbara Goodstein, a founding partner of R360, notes that this liquidity positions them well for potential future opportunities. The sentiment among these investors is one of patience and strategic foresight, as they remain committed to long-term investment horizons despite current market fluctuations.

Similarly, financial advisors like Jason Katz from UBS and Christopher Keller from Fifth Third Bank highlight a cautious yet opportunistic approach among their clients. With interest rates on 10-year Treasuries rising to 4.3%, there is renewed interest in fixed-income investments. Investors are weighing the balance between holding cash, which currently earns around 3%, and exploring opportunities in the Treasury market, indicating a thoughtful navigation of the current economic landscape amidst uncertainty.
social_text: After the S&P 500's worst month since March 2025, high net worth investors are holding up to 30% in cash, seeking opportunities in volatile markets, and exploring 10-year Treasuries at 4.3% for strategic investments.

title: Morgan Stanley says buy this dividend-paying ‘key beneficiary’ of rising oil prices
summary:
Morgan Stanley has identified Chord Energy as a significant beneficiary of rising oil prices, which have surged due to ongoing geopolitical tensions, particularly in the Middle East. With Brent crude oil futures experiencing a remarkable increase of over 60% in March, the firm anticipates that oil prices will stabilize above pre-conflict levels even if tensions ease. This forecast is supported by the need to replenish oil inventories and concerns regarding supply security, leading to a projected average price of $80 per barrel for West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude in 2026, with a slight decrease to $70 in 2027. 

In light of these developments, Morgan Stanley has upgraded Chord Energy's stock rating from equal weight to overweight, raising its price target from $114 to $168, indicating a potential upside of nearly 15% from its recent closing price. The analysts highlighted that Chord Energy stands out among its peers due to its robust free cash flow and shareholder returns, with an 18% free cash flow yield compared to the industry average of 12%. Additionally, the company is expected to deliver a 12% shareholder return yield, significantly higher than the average of 6% for its competitors.

Despite the challenges posed by rising energy prices for consumers, Chord Energy's strong performance in the market, with a year-to-date increase of over 53%, positions it favorably for investors looking for opportunities in the oil exploration and production sector. title: 3 things Nike investors need to watch with the stock plunging after earnings
summary:
Nike's stock has experienced a significant decline, dropping over 10% in premarket trading following the release of its fiscal third-quarter earnings. Despite reporting better-than-expected earnings, investors are reacting negatively due to several concerning indicators. A primary issue is the continued downturn in Nike's China business, where sales fell by 10% year-over-year. Digital sales in the region saw a staggering 21% decrease, and wholesale sales dropped by 13%. CEO Elliott Hill acknowledged these challenges and emphasized the need for a more localized approach to reconnect with consumers in China, indicating a strategic shift to address these market dynamics.

In addition to the struggles in China, Nike's guidance for the upcoming fiscal fourth quarter has raised alarms among analysts. The company anticipates a sales decline of 2% to 4% and a reduction in gross profit margins by 25 to 75 basis points. Citi analyst Paul Lejuez noted that this guidance translates to an earnings per share estimate of only $0.05 to $0.15, which falls short of the consensus forecast of $0.20. This discrepancy has led to increased scrutiny from investors, many of whom had previously expected stronger earnings.

Looking ahead, analysts are urging caution as Nike navigates these challenges. Jefferies analyst Randy Konik described the situation as a 'work in progress,' suggesting that patience will be necessary as the company implements its strategies to stabilize sales and improve profitability. Investors are advised to closely monitor Nike's performance in China, its sales guidance, and the overall market response as the company seeks to regain momentum in a competitive landscape.
social_text: Nike's stock plummeted over 10% after earnings, with China sales down 10% and digital sales down 21%. Q4 guidance suggests EPS of $0.05 to $0.15, below consensus of $0.20, indicating ongoing challenges.

title: Monzo to close US operations, accounts
summary:
Monzo, the U.K.-based fintech, has announced the closure of its U.S. operations, a significant shift in strategy as it aims to focus on its core markets in the U.K. and Europe. The decision comes alongside the layoff of approximately 50 employees and the cessation of onboarding new U.S. customers. Existing U.S. users will still have access to their accounts until June, but the company is clearly pivoting away from the American market. This move reflects a broader trend among fintech companies, as many are seeking to capitalize on regulatory changes and growth opportunities in their home regions.

The closure of Monzo's U.S. operations is particularly notable given the competitive landscape in the fintech sector. While Monzo is withdrawing, other companies like Revolut, Nubank, and Bunq are actively pursuing U.S. banking licenses, indicating a contrasting approach to market expansion. Monzo's decision to step back from the U.S. market is a calculated one, driven by its recent acquisition of a full banking license from the European Central Bank and the Central Bank of Ireland, which allows for greater growth potential in Europe.

This strategic shift underscores Monzo's commitment to scaling its operations where it sees the most promise. The company previously attempted to enter the U.S. market by applying for a banking license in 2020 but withdrew after 17 months of waiting. With a robust customer base of 15 million in the U.K., Monzo is now prioritizing its resources to enhance its offerings and expand its presence in Europe, rather than competing in the challenging U.S. landscape.
social_text: Monzo is closing its U.S. operations, laying off 50 employees, and halting new customer onboarding. The fintech aims to focus on its 15M UK customers and European growth after receiving a full banking license.

title: What to Expect From Digital Realty Trust's Q1 2026 Earnings Report

title: Nuvve Holding Corp. Q4 2025 Earnings Call Summary
summary:

To enhance operational efficiency and reduce costs, Nuvve is integrating end-to-end AI-based functionalities across various business processes, including project management, sales support, and finance. title: B.O.S. Better Online Solutions Ltd. Q4 2025 Earnings Call Summary
summary:
B.O.S. Better Online Solutions Ltd. reported impressive financial results for Q4 2025, showcasing a 27% increase in revenue, reaching $51 million. This growth was primarily driven by strong demand in the defense sector, particularly within their Supply Chain and Robotics divisions. The company has successfully leveraged operational efficiencies, resulting in a net income surge of 57% to $3.6 million. This performance highlights B.O.S.'s ability to scale profitably, even amidst ongoing geopolitical challenges that have impacted various markets.

The Supply Chain and Robotics divisions have benefited from a 'self-replenishing' product flow, as manufacturing partners continue to invest in next-generation technologies. However, the geopolitical tensions in Israel, which escalated in October 2023, have adversely affected the commercial RFID market, leading to cumulative goodwill impairment charges of $1.9 million over the past two years. In response to these challenges, B.O.S. has strategically pivoted its RFID division to focus on the more stable Israeli hospital vertical, aiming to mitigate risks associated with volatile civil market cycles.

Additionally, B.O.S. has established a formal representative agency in India to tap into the growing subcontracting opportunities for global defense programs, including partnerships with major players like Boeing and Israel Aerospace Industries (IAI). This strategic move is expected to enhance the company's presence in the defense sector and capitalize on emerging opportunities in the region, further solidifying its market position and growth trajectory in the coming years.

title: 374Water targets growth in 2026 with scaled deployments of waste destruction technology

title: Morgan Stanley’s defensive playbook for spiking oil prices amid Iran war
summary:
In light of escalating tensions due to the Iran war, Morgan Stanley has advised investors to adopt a defensive stance in their portfolios. Following President Trump's remarks about intensifying actions against Tehran, oil prices surged dramatically, with West Texas Intermediate crude futures climbing over 11% to settle at $111.54, marking the highest close since June 2022. Similarly, Brent crude futures rose by 7.78% to $109.03. This volatility in oil prices has raised concerns about energy supply disruptions, prompting Morgan Stanley's strategists to reassess their outlook on risk assets, suggesting that the potential for downside has increased significantly.

The firm has downgraded its recommendation on global equities from overweight to equal weight, particularly reducing exposure to emerging markets. Their revised asset allocation now includes a 55% weighting in equities, with specific allocations of 32% to the U.S., 10% to Europe, 5% to Japan, and 8% to emerging markets. Morgan Stanley's strategists highlighted that while the earnings trajectory was strong prior to the conflict, the anticipated rise in energy prices and geopolitical risks are likely to negatively impact both earnings and market valuations.

Additionally, the strategists have allocated 25% of the portfolio to core fixed income, with 20% in government bonds and 5% in other fixed income assets. They noted that while Brazil may offer some opportunities, many Asian emerging markets remain vulnerable due to their reliance on Middle Eastern oil and gas supplies. This strategic pivot underscores the importance of navigating the current market landscape with caution amid geopolitical uncertainties.
social_text: Morgan Stanley advises a defensive investment strategy as oil prices soar due to the Iran war, with WTI crude at $111.54 (+11%) and Brent at $109.03 (+7.78%). They downgrade global equities and increase cash holdings to mitigate risks.

title: This JPMorgan income ETF was named one of the best by Morningstar. Where its manager is investing now
summary:
The JPMorgan Dividend Leaders ETF (JDIV) has gained recognition as one of the top high-dividend ETFs for passive income, as highlighted by Morningstar. With approximately 51% of its assets allocated to U.S. stocks and the remainder diversified globally, JDIV aims to provide investors with a robust income stream while mitigating risks associated with market volatility. As of the latest reports, JDIV has achieved a total return of -1.43% year to date, outperforming the S&P 500, which has seen a decline of -4.33%. This performance underscores the ETF's strategic positioning in the current market landscape, where dividend stocks are increasingly favored by investors seeking stability amidst uncertainty.

Portfolio manager Sam Witherow emphasizes JDIV's unique approach to stock selection, which combines a core market experience with a slight value tilt. The ETF's benchmark, the MSCI ACWI Index, encompasses large- and mid-cap stocks from both developed and emerging markets. JDIV boasts a dividend yield of 2.28%, significantly higher than the benchmark's 1.64%. With an expense ratio of just 0.47%, the fund is designed to deliver not only competitive returns but also faster dividend growth, projected at 8% over the next five years, compared to the global average of 7%.

Witherow's strategy focuses on providing a material premium in dividend income, appealing to clients who prioritize income stability over maximum market upside. He notes that while companies tend to cut dividends during crises, they are often slow to reinstate them, creating a favorable environment for JDIV's growth potential. As the ETF continues to navigate the complexities of the global market, its emphasis on rigorous stock selection and dividend growth positions it as a compelling option for income-focused investors looking to diversify their portfolios beyond U.S. equities.

title: Artemis II is NASA’s last Moon mission without Silicon Valley
summary:
NASA's Artemis II mission marks a significant milestone as it sends astronauts back to the Moon for the first time in over half a century. This mission is particularly notable as it represents the last time NASA will undertake such an endeavor without substantial support from Silicon Valley tech companies. The Artemis program has its roots in the second Bush administration, which initiated the development of the Space Launch System (SLS) and the Orion spacecraft. However, as costs escalated and timelines extended, NASA pivoted towards collaborating with private companies, ultimately leading to a partnership with SpaceX that has transformed the landscape of space exploration.

The SLS, currently the most powerful operational rocket, has only flown once prior to this mission, which is set to establish a new record for human exploration in the solar system. Despite the technological advancements and the successful launch of the SLS, the program has faced criticism for being costly and inefficient compared to the rapid progress made by private companies like SpaceX and Blue Origin. These companies have revolutionized space travel with their reusable rockets and have attracted significant venture capital investment, creating a competitive environment for lunar exploration.

Looking ahead, the pressure is mounting on SpaceX and Blue Origin to lead the next phase of lunar missions. As NASA continues its Artemis program, the reliance on traditional contractors like Boeing and Lockheed Martin may diminish, paving the way for a new era of space exploration driven by private innovation. The Artemis II mission not only highlights NASA's commitment to returning to the Moon but also underscores the shifting dynamics in the space industry, where private companies are poised to play a pivotal role in future missions.
social_text: NASA's Artemis II mission sends astronauts to the Moon for the first time in 54 years, marking the last mission without major Silicon Valley support. The SLS, developed by Boeing and Lockheed Martin, faces competition from SpaceX and Blue Origin.

(NYSE:HD) has made a significant move in the HVAC distribution sector by acquiring Mingledorff’s, a well-established distributor with 42 locations across five southeastern U.S. states. Importantly, the leadership team at Mingledorff’s will remain intact post-acquisition, which is likely to ensure a smooth transition and continuity in operations. This move aligns with Home Depot's broader strategy to enhance its service offerings, as evidenced by its recent announcement of a real-time delivery tracker for large materials, which will provide customers with minute-by-minute updates via GPS technology.

As Home Depot continues to innovate and expand its market reach, the implications of this acquisition could be far-reaching. By strengthening its foothold in HVAC distribution, Home Depot not only diversifies its product offerings but also positions itself to capitalize on the growing demand for HVAC solutions. In terms of future outlook, Enerpac has revised its fiscal year 2026 adjusted EPS guidance to a range of $1.86 to $1.92, down from the previous estimate of $1.85 to $2.00, while the consensus remains at $1.92. The company has also tightened its revenue forecast to between $635 million and $650 million, compared to the earlier range of $635 million to $655 million, with the consensus at $637.17 million. title: Selling pressure for chip stocks is nearing exhaustion and a turnaround could be in the cards, charts show
summary:
The current market sentiment surrounding chip stocks is heavily bearish, with many investors feeling the pressure of declining prices. However, historical trends suggest that sustainable market lows often occur before any improvement in sentiment or fundamental data. The phrase 'it's darkest before dawn' rings true in this context, as analysts observe potential signs of a turnaround in the semiconductor sector. The VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) has shown a distinct A-B-C correction pattern, indicating that selling pressure may be nearing its peak. title: The S&P 500 is wrapping up a tough month. Why April could be better
summary:
The S&P 500 has faced a challenging March, with a decline of over 5%, marking its worst monthly performance since March 2025. This downturn is attributed to various factors, including geopolitical tensions, rising oil prices, and persistent inflation concerns. However, historical data suggests that April could offer a much-needed rebound for investors. Historically, April ranks as the second-best month for the S&P 500, averaging gains of 1.4%, and has consistently outperformed other indices like the Nasdaq Composite and Russell 2000. Jeffrey Hirsch, editor-in-chief of the Stock Trader's Almanac, emphasizes that April is rarely a dangerous month for the index, with only a few exceptions in recent years. The potential for recovery in April is particularly significant following the pressures of the previous month, as investors look for signs of stability and growth in the market.

Despite the optimistic outlook for April, there are underlying concerns that could affect market performance. The ongoing U.S.-Iran conflict and its implications for global oil prices, along with the uncertainty surrounding midterm elections, could introduce volatility. Historically, the S&P 500 has shown an average decline of 0.3% during April in midterm election years, which could dampen investor sentiment. Hirsch notes that the second and third quarters of midterm years tend to be the weakest periods in the election cycle, suggesting that while April may offer some recovery, caution is warranted as the year progresses.

In summary, while the S&P 500's performance in April may provide a glimmer of hope following a tough March, investors should remain vigilant about external factors that could impact market dynamics. The historical trends indicate that April is typically a strong month, but the potential for midterm election volatility and geopolitical tensions could temper expectations. As the market navigates these challenges, the focus will be on how external events unfold and their influence on investor confidence and stock performance in the coming months.
social_text: The S&P 500 is down over 5% in March, its worst month since March 2025. Historically, April averages gains of 1.4%, but midterm elections may introduce volatility, with a 0.3% average decline in April during these years.

Among the 11 sectors of the S&P 500, only the energy sector managed to finish the month on a positive note, driven by rising oil prices linked to the Middle East conflict. Many stocks faced considerable declines, with analysts now predicting potential rebounds for several of them.

CNBC identified a group of S&P 500 stocks that have been particularly hard hit but are expected to recover. To qualify for this list, stocks needed to have dropped at least 20% in March, received a buy rating from over 51% of analysts, and have an average price target suggesting at least a 35% upside. Notable mentions include Carnival, which saw a 24% decline, and Micron Technology, which fell by 22%. Analysts are optimistic about Carnival's recovery, with 74% of them maintaining a bullish outlook and an average price target suggesting a potential 45% increase in share value.

HSBC recently upgraded Carnival's stock rating from hold to buy, citing a favorable risk-reward scenario despite near-term earnings uncertainties. Analyst Meredith Prichard Jensen noted that Carnival shares are currently trading at a significant discount compared to historical multiples. Similarly, Micron's stock is also viewed positively, with analysts projecting a rebound as market conditions stabilize. Overall, the sentiment among analysts indicates a cautious optimism for these stocks as they navigate the current market challenges.

title: OpenAI acquires TBPN, the buzzy founder-led business talk show
summary:
OpenAI has made a strategic move by acquiring the Technology Business Programming Network (TBPN), marking its first foray into the media sector. TBPN, a daily live talk show hosted by former tech founders John Coogan and Jordi Hays, has established itself as a prominent platform for discussions on technology, business, AI, and defense. The show has garnered a loyal following in Silicon Valley, attracting influential figures such as Mark Zuckerberg, Satya Nadella, and Marc Benioff, who engage in candid conversations about current events and industry trends. This acquisition aligns with OpenAI's mission to broaden its outreach and enhance public understanding of AI's impact on everyday life.

The acquisition is expected to bolster TBPN's already impressive growth trajectory, with projections indicating that the show will generate over $30 million in revenue this year. OpenAI plans to maintain TBPN's brand identity while leveraging its founders' expertise in communications and marketing to further scale the platform. This approach reflects OpenAI's recognition of the unique challenges it faces in communicating complex AI concepts to a broader audience, as highlighted by Fidji Simo, OpenAI's head of AGI deployment.

By integrating TBPN into its operations, OpenAI aims to create a more engaging dialogue around AI technologies and their implications. The company already produces a podcast featuring long-form discussions with its own team members, but the addition of TBPN will provide a dynamic platform for real-time interactions and insights from industry leaders. This acquisition not only enhances OpenAI's media presence but also positions it to play a pivotal role in shaping the narrative around AI advancements and their societal impact.

title: Flipboard’s new ‘social websites’ help publishers and creators tap into the open social web
summary:
Flipboard has introduced a new feature called 'social websites' that allows creators and publishers to establish their own dedicated spaces on the open social web. This innovation aims to consolidate various forms of content, including social media posts, videos, podcasts, and newsletters, into a single platform that creators can control. By integrating profiles and posts from decentralized platforms like Mastodon and Bluesky, as well as traditional content sources, Flipboard is positioning itself as a facilitator of community-driven content curation.

The concept of social websites is rooted in the idea of giving creators more ownership over their content and the conversations surrounding it. Flipboard's CEO, Mike McCue, emphasized that this new model enables podcasters, creators, and publications to build communities around their work without starting from scratch. Instead, they can leverage existing conversations and audiences across various platforms, thereby enhancing engagement and interaction with their content.

This launch also marks the first web-based extension of Flipboard's Surf app, which allows users to explore the open social web. Notable publishers such as The Verge, WIRED, and Rolling Stone have already adopted social websites, creating hubs where readers can follow their favorite journalists and content creators. This initiative reflects Flipboard's commitment to embracing decentralized social media and providing tools that empower creators and publishers in the evolving digital landscape.
social_text: Flipboard's new social websites enable creators to consolidate content from platforms like Mastodon and Bluesky into a single hub, enhancing community engagement and control over conversations. Major publishers like WIRED and Rolling Stone are already on board.

title: ElevenLabs releases a new AI-powered music-generation app
summary:
ElevenLabs, a prominent voice AI company, has launched an innovative iOS application named ElevenMusic, designed to generate and discover AI-created music. This strategic move, which was officially released on April 1, aims to position ElevenLabs beyond its initial focus on voice modeling, as it seeks to diversify its offerings in the competitive landscape of AI-driven music platforms. ElevenMusic allows users to create up to seven songs daily using natural language prompts, providing customization options such as song length, lyrics, and writing style. The app also features a social aspect, enabling users to discover and remix tracks created by others, fostering a collaborative environment for music creation.

The app includes various functionalities akin to established music streaming services, featuring live stations, curated albums, and mood-based mixes. Users can explore sections like top charts and trending releases, enhancing the user experience. ElevenLabs offers a Pro subscription tier priced at $9.99 per month or $95.90 annually, which significantly increases the track creation limit to 500 songs per month and provides over 500 GB of storage. This tier also unlocks access to a wider array of musical styles and moods, catering to more serious creators.

Having recently raised $500 million in a Series C funding round, valuing the company at $11 billion, ElevenLabs is aggressively expanding its portfolio of creative tools. The company has previously released a commercially safe music-generation model and collaborated with renowned music producers to create an AI-assisted album. This latest app release is part of ElevenLabs' broader strategy to innovate within the AI space, offering a comprehensive suite of creative solutions that includes ad generation, voice cloning, and more, thereby solidifying its position in the rapidly evolving tech landscape.
social_text: ElevenLabs launches ElevenMusic, an iOS app for AI-generated music, allowing users to create 7 songs daily. Pro tier at $9.99/month boosts limit to 500 tracks. The company raised $500M at an $11B valuation.
